86.reason: Operator precedence: `||` has lower precedence than `&&`, so the condition is `(sign2 == 1 && input.charAt(pos)!='*') || (sign1 ==1)`. This causes the loop to run whenever sign1 is 1, even if sign2 is 0, and the loop body does not check for `*` to exit; it only increments pos. This can lead to an infinite loop or skipping characters incorrectly, especially when sign1 is set from a previous line.
